tweets.text:
Hi, I’m Joshua Lorcy!

Pokemon VGC player & Community Ambassador with 6 years of tournament experience and 3 years casting grassroots and unofficial Pokemon events.

I enjoy breaking down gameplay while keeping broadcasts engaging for both new and experienced viewers.

DMs open!

And Excadrill provides a fast offensive option that is good into both Zard Y AND faries. 

By doubling up on roles offensively and defensively you have a lot more flexibility and security in the matchups

Gholdengo doesn’t like Incin? You’ve got Raptor + Milo + Ttar (7/1?)

And Staraptor covers this really well!

The other thing about this team is that the support pieces also synergize and cover the weakness of the team super well

Milotic wrecks Incin & Garchomp and provides speed control with icy wind

Sinistcha is healing and redirection (6/1?)

And that’s where the 3rd piece of the Puzzle Ttar comes in

Nothing ruins Zard-Y’s day quite like Ttar between the ability to reset the weather on both switching AND Mega Evolution. And of course Rock Slide destroys him

But Ttar hates intimidate, Garchomp, Gambit etc (5/1?)

And Staraptor gets DESTROYED by fairies — but with Intim + Contrary and Stab CC it’s actually good into Gambit, Garchomp, Incin, and even helps into Basc

Gholdengo covers the Faries and Ghost types for you the synergy is near perfect - except Zard Y is still scary (4/1?)

Each Mon has some unique strengths — paired with some common weaknesses. But these mons patch eachother’s weaknesses uniquely well!

Let’s start with Gholdengo. It DESTROYS Faries but hates Incin, Zard, Garchomp, Gambit, even Basc — you know the most common guys (3/1?)

Team Spotlight: Staraptor Sand!

Yes you heard that right! This team created by @lovejapanfrombr is one of the coolest teams out there and seems uniquely poised to take on a meta full of Sun, Rain, and even both at once! 

Let’s talk about what makes this unique team work! (1/1?)
